steps.title: Find the mass per object
steps.content: Given that $r$ identical objects have total mass $t$ kilograms, the mass of a single object is $\frac{t}{r}$ kilograms.

steps.title: Calculate the mass of 146r objects
steps.content:
If one object has mass $\frac{t}{r}$, then the mass of $146r$ objects is:
$$146r \cdot \frac{t}{r} = 146t$$
The $r$ cancels out. That matches choice D — D is correct.
